issues: codex interleaves its native `exec` shell (4x) with the bridge tool `execute_shell_command` (3x). The bridge path genuinely ran (3 execute_shell_command calls read+write+read report.md), but the run is codex-shell + bridge rather than bridge-only — the executor config does not disable codex's native shell. Non-blocking; same observation as the provider-layer codex streaming test.
agent_review.notes: PRODUCTION-FIDELITY, RICH TASK: codex ran the real 3-step file workflow through the REAL bridge — execute_shell_command (via mcpbridge → executor → real shell) read build_id.txt, wrote report.md, and read it back. The build id BUILD_ID_246be08b814f was only in the file, and report.md on disk is a correct markdown table containing it. codex's final reply is a clean GitHub-flavored markdown table ('| Field | Value | ... | build_id | BUILD_ID_246be08b814f | ... | status | ok |') that streamed as clean content (Source != terminal), no ANSI noise. Signed good; the exec/bridge interleave above is the tracked caveat.
